title: RunContext (lifecycle)
description: Run lifecycle/approval metadata half of the split RunContext contract, owned by forge-console-api, per docs/architecture/data-ownership.md:39 and docs/architecture/contract-catalog.md:24.
$comment: compat_class: open (plan §2 row 'context/run-context-lifecycle'; plan §5 acceptance matrix 'RunContext(=2파일, catalog_name 역참조) | lifecycle open / experiment closed'). Authority: approved plan §1.1 R10 (RunContext split into 2 files, A안 confirmed — registry ties them back together via a shared catalog_name: 'RunContext' field, NOT authored in this schema file itself, see packages/contracts/registry.json ownership, out of scope for this unit); data-ownership.md:39 ('RunContext는 분할 소유: run lifecycle/승인 메타 = forge-console-api / 실험 설계 파라미터... = experiment-attribution'); contract-catalog.md:24 (owner split, idempotency key run_id, sensitivity customer-proprietary meta, retention 'run 후 재현성 장기; secret 참조 금지'); approved Wave 1 plan §1.1 R10 + packages/contracts/registry.schema.json (catalog_name field) record the back-reference convention. The split JOIN KEY between this file and context/run-context-experiment/v1 is run_id (data-ownership.md:39).

run_id.$comment: tenancy-model.md:42 Identifier set — 'single FORGE run'. Format OPEN (issuer runtime convention, W1 opaque) per common/identifiers $defs.run_id. This is also the join key back to context/run-context-experiment/v1 (data-ownership.md:39).

tenant_id.$comment: Tenant discriminator — every run belongs to exactly one tenant (tenancy-model.md hard isolation boundary).

state.$comment: Verbatim from docs/specs/SAENA_k3s_Package_and_Operations_Spec_v1.md §4.3 '상태 전이' stateDiagram-v2 (lines 216-233 as of the cited version) — 11 states, copied exactly including case and underscores. Terminal states per the diagram are HANDOFF_READY, FAILED, and CANCELLED (all transition to [*]); WAITING_APPROVAL branches to EXECUTING on 'approved' or CANCELLED on 'rejected' (B-department signed approval only, per k3s spec §4.3 note: 'Policy Gate와 Temporal workflow가 이 상태 전이를 각각 검증한다'). This schema intentionally validates membership in the state SET only — it does NOT encode the transition graph/edges; illegal transitions (e.g. INTAKE -> HANDOFF_READY directly) are a runtime/workflow-engine obligation, not a JSON Schema structural constraint. ENUM REVISION RULE: any change to this list is a MAJOR version bump (ADR-0012 enum-widening-is-major rule, same logic applied to common/engine-id) AND MUST stay byte-for-byte in sync with k3s spec §4.3 — if this list and the k3s spec ever diverge, the k3s spec is authoritative and this schema is out of date.

base_commit.$comment: approved plan §2 row — 40-hex git commit SHA-1, IMMUTABLE once the run is created (the commit the run's discovery/plan/execution is anchored to does not change mid-run).

human_approval_required.$comment: docs/specs/SAENA_B_Department_Agent_Prompt_Package_v1.md §1:59 — whether this run requires an explicit human/B-department signed approval before the WAITING_APPROVAL -> EXECUTING transition (k3s spec §4.3: 'WAITING_APPROVAL에서 EXECUTING으로 넘어가는 요청은 B부서 signed approval만 가능하다').
